title: Tillandsias Runtime Log Schema - Golden Reference
description: Stable log field schema for backwards compatibility. Core fields are required and immutable. Optional fields may be added without version bump. Breaking changes require version increment and migration path.

schema_version.description: Schema version for backwards compatibility. Immutable. Incremented only on breaking changes.

timestamp.description: RFC3339 timestamp of log event emission

accountability.description: Flag indicating this is a sensitive/auditable operation requiring traceability

safety.example: Container network sandboxed from external IPs

context.description: Arbitrary context fields (key-value pairs) for additional event metadata

sample_rate.description: Sampling rate applied to this trace (e.g., 0.5 for 50% sampled). Only set if trace was subject to cost-aware sampling.

span_id.description: Span ID for distributed tracing across containers. Enables cross-container log linkage.

parent_span_id.description: Parent span ID for cross-container log linkage. Establishes parent-child relationship in distributed traces.

trace_id.description: Trace ID shared across all spans in a distributed trace. Correlates all related spans.

stability_notes.backwards_compatibility: All core_fields are immutable. Removing or renaming any core_field is a breaking change that requires a major version bump.
stability_notes.forward_compatibility: New optional_fields may be added to LogEntry without version bump. Consumers must gracefully ignore unknown fields.

breaking_changes.definition: Any of: field rename, field type change, enum value change, core_field removal, required->optional flip
breaking_changes.migration_protocol: 1. Increment schema_version (1.0 -> 1.1). 2. Add migration notes to CHANGELOG.md. 3. Broadcast to all known log consumers.

field_type_constraints.timestamp: Must be RFC3339 string (ISO8601). Always UTC. Represents moment event was emitted.
field_type_constraints.level: Strictly enumerated. TRACE=most verbose, ERROR=least verbose. Never add new variants without deprecation period.
field_type_constraints.component: String. Examples: 'forge', 'proxy', 'git-service', 'inference'. Case-sensitive. Slugified (lowercase, hyphens OK).
field_type_constraints.message: String. Plain text, no embedded JSON. Redacted for sensitive fields per OWASP guidance.
field_type_constraints.accountability: Boolean. True indicates traceability-critical operation. False (default) for routine logs.
field_type_constraints.safety: String. Human-readable safety note. Example: 'Forge container isolated from external network.'
field_type_constraints.spec_trace: String. Format: spec-name (slug). Example: 'native-secrets-store'. Maps to @trace spec:<name> in source.
field_type_constraints.context: Object. Arbitrary key-value pairs. No schema constraints. Values may be string, number, boolean, null, or nested objects/arrays.
field_type_constraints.sample_rate: Number (float). Range: 0.0 to 1.0. Represents percentage of traces sampled. Only present if trace was cost-sampled.
field_type_constraints.span_id: String. Hexadecimal trace span identifier. Uniquely identifies a span within a trace. Example: '0123456789abcdef'.
field_type_constraints.parent_span_id: String. Hexadecimal trace span identifier. References parent span in distributed trace. Omitted for root spans.
field_type_constraints.trace_id: String. UUID-format identifier shared across all spans in a distributed trace. Enables cross-container log correlation.

accountability_event.safety: Token isolated in ephemeral podman secret, readable only by git-service container
